title: Unidentified Kenobi baby
content:
{{Top}} {{Conjecture}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Unidentified Kenobi baby |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender|Male]]{{Kenobi|Part III}} |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Kenobi family]] |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=[[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] {{C|alleged brother}} |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I remember a baby.''"
"''A baby?''"
"''Yes, I think I had a brother. Really don't remember him. I wished I did. Then I joined the Jedi, and I got a new family just like you.|Obi-Wan Kenobi and [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]]|}} A '''baby''' was a [[Kenobi family|relative]] to the [[Force-sensitive]] [[human]] [[Gender|male]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] prior to joining the [[Jedi Order]]. Although he was taken from his family when he was young, Obi-Wan still had vague memories, glimpses and flashes of his past, notably remembering his [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's mother|mother]]'s shawl, his [[Obi-Wan Kenobi's father|father]]'s hands, and a [[Youngling|baby]]. Obi-Wan believed the baby to be his brother, as he once told young [[Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]], and wished he did remember more about him. {{Char-stub}} [[File:ObiWan9ABY.png|200px|thumb|right|Although Obi-Wan Kenobi didn't remember his brother other than glimpses and flashes of his family, he wished he did.]] ==Behind the scenes== Obi-Wan Kenobi's sibling was first mentioned in "[[Part III]]" of the [[Disney+]] series [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi|''Obi-Wan Kenobi'']], which premiered on [[June 1]], [[2022]].{{D23|url=everything-new-you-can-stream-on-disney-in-june-2022|text=Everything New You Can Stream on Disney+ in June 2022}} In the script of the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], the last installment of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[original trilogy]], it was originally suggested that Kenobi's brother was [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' an idea that was included in the film's [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]] by [[James Kahn]],[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] though the finished film did not include it.{{Film|VI}} This would be acknowledged in later [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] works. The [[1999]] junior novel ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Hidden Past]]'' included Kenobi experiencing a vision of his childhood, including a brother named [[Owen (Kenobi)|Owen]],''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Hidden Past]]'' however the [[2002]] [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] established that Lars and Kenobi were not biologically related, and that Lars was instead [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] step-brother.{{Film|II}} The [[2015]] short story "[[Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke]]" by [[Abel G. Peña]] later suggested that Kenobi's earlier vision had in fact been of his future life on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], living in [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] with some interaction with Lars.{{StoryCite|story=Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke|book=SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story}} ==Appearances== *{{Kenobi|Part III}} {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Hermano de Obi-Wan Kenobi |fr=Frère d'Obi-Wan Kenobi |ka=კენობიების უცნობი ბავშვი |pl=Brat Obi-Wana Kenobiego }} [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Kenobi family]] [[Category:Males]]
